indices.name: S&P 500
indices.original_text: The S&P 500 was up 0.4%. At nearly 6979, it's the closest the index has finished a day to the 7000 mark. The main stock market index of United States, the US500, rose to 6993 points on January 27, 2026.

sources.name: Barron's
sources.original_text: The S&P 500 was up 0.4%. At nearly 6979, it's the closest the index has finished a day to the 7000 mark.

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The main stock market index of United States, the US500, rose to 6993 points on January 27, 2026, gaining 0.62% from the previous session.

indices.original_text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 409 points, or 0.8%. Dow Jones fell about 500 points, dragged lower by a 20% plunge in UnitedHealth shares.

indices.name: KOSPI
indices.original_text: South Korea's main stock market index, the KOSPI, rose to 5085 points on January 27, 2026, gaining 2.73% from the previous session.

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The benchmark KOSPI rose 2.73% to close at 5,084 on Tuesday. South Korea's main stock market index, the KOSPI, rose to 5085 points on January 27, 2026, gaining 2.73% from the previous session.

cross_verification.note: KOSPI 5,085 (Jan 27) is record high. Prior close 4,990 (Jan 22) shows +1.9% gain consistent with reported +2.73%

indices.name: KOSDAQ
indices.original_text: KOSDAQ closed at 1,064.41, up 70.48 points (7.09%) from the previous session. This represents the highest level in 25 years and 5 months since 1,074.10 points on Sept.6, 2000.

sources.name: Business Korea
sources.original_text: KOSDAQ closed at 1,064.41, up 70.48 points (7.09%) from the previous session. Based on closing prices, this represents the highest level in 25 years and 5 months since 1,074.10 points on Sept.6, 2000.

sources.name: Investing.com (UK)
sources.original_text: The KOSDAQ Index price today is 1,010.00.

sources.name: Korea JoongAng Daily
sources.original_text: A sidecar was triggered on the Kosdaq market Monday after a sharp surge in prices.

cross_verification.note: KOSDAQ 1,064 confirmed as 25-year high. Jump from 1,010 to 1,064 represents ~5.3% gain, consistent with 7.09% reported.

fx.name: DXY (Dollar Index)
fx.original_text: DXY decreased to 96.16, the lowest since February 2022. Dollar Index Hits Near 4-year Low. The dollar weakened for a fifth straight session to 96 on Wednesday.

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: DXY decreased to 96.16, the lowest since February 2022. Over the past 4 weeks, Dollar Index lost 1.89%, and in the last 12 months, it decreased 10.83%.

sources.name: MarketPulse
sources.original_text: The Dollar Index (DXY), which had already corrected by 3% since last Monday, just dropped another percent to 4-year lows, now below 96.00.

sources.name: MUFG Research
sources.original_text: The broad US dollar index (DXY) remains under downward pressure, having slipped below the 98.00 handle.

cross_verification.note: DXY 96 confirmed at 4-year lows. Multiple sources confirm sharp dollar decline.
